meaning: adj.深思熟虑的,故意的,预有准备的v.商讨
examples: This was a deliberate piece of misinformation.
Witnesses say the firing was deliberate and sustained.
The deliberate cruelty of his words cut her like a knife.
Its slow-motion, pythonesque squeeze was initially inadvertent, but then, about two years ago, deliberate.
When going up against zombies with a slow and deliberate trod, the Delta Six got the job done.
